title: Pay Pal

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer encounters some mishap (opening frustration)
jokes.punchline: His iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and iconic quotability, but routine usage

jokes.text: Mom, it's not funny if I can't hear the cats drown in their own barf.
jokes.setup: Child complaining about TV volume being too low
jokes.punchline: The disturbing specificity of what he wants to hear clearly
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart's dark humor - perfectly in character with disturbing but funny specificity

jokes.text: Why can't kids still watch Captain Kangaroo?!
jokes.setup: Reacting to Bart's disturbing TV preferences
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic plea for wholesome children's programming
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Marge's values, but fairly predictable response

jokes.text: Culture's in decline. Deal with it. Oh!
jokes.setup: Marge's plea for wholesome TV
jokes.punchline: Lisa's blunt, fatalistic assessment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa - intellectually precocious but cynical, delivered with characteristic directness

jokes.text: Yay! A mess! Ooh! Can I lick the ceiling?
jokes.setup: Cake falls on ceiling
jokes.punchline: Baby's excitement about mess and bizarre request
jokes.explanation: Good baby humor - innocent excitement about chaos, appropriately weird request

jokes.text: Now there's no choice but to go... store-bought.
jokes.setup: Homemade cake is ruined
jokes.punchline: Dramatic pause before admitting to the ultimate shame for a homemaker
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - treating store-bought cake as a moral failing with appropriate gravity

jokes.text: I need a price check on Gramma Shortcut's Store-bought Bundt Cake.
jokes.setup: Marge's shame about buying store-bought
jokes.punchline: Brand name that literally advertises laziness
jokes.explanation: Clever fake product name that makes the subtext text - good background gag

jokes.text: Keep it! Let's go!
jokes.setup: Public humiliation from price check announcement
jokes.punchline: Marge's mortification drives her to abandon the cake entirely
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Marge's shame - public announcement was the final straw

jokes.text: You know, I used to be the buyer for the whole chain-- 71 stores. You heard of the A&P? Well, we used to be the J&G.
jokes.setup: Employee shares unsolicited career history
jokes.punchline: Pathetic comparison to failed grocery chain with worse letters
jokes.explanation: Good sad-sack character moment - the decline from A&P to J&G captures failed ambition

jokes.text: There used to be three, but we lost the 'Z'
jokes.setup: J&G grocery chain backstory
jokes.punchline: Implication that losing one letter somehow destroyed the business
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic - treating a letter as if it's crucial business infrastructure

jokes.text: If I make out with you, will you shut up?
jokes.setup: Old man's relentless nostalgic rambling
jokes.punchline: Homer's desperate, inappropriate negotiation tactic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer desperation move - willing to sacrifice dignity for peace, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Remember green stamps? Ours was gray.
jokes.setup: Continuing his pathetic business nostalgia
jokes.punchline: Even their customer rewards were inferior and depressing
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of business failure - gray stamps suggest depression and failure

jokes.text: We must have the same recipe. Drive to store, buy cheap cake, serves them right.
jokes.setup: Neighbor compliments similar-looking store cake
jokes.punchline: Marge's bitter acknowledgment that everyone takes shortcuts
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment - Marge's guilt dissolved by realizing everyone's a fraud

jokes.text: game night on Saturday next-- or as you call it, next Saturday.
jokes.setup: British neighbor making social invitation
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily correcting his own perfectly normal phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - pretentious British neighbor overcorrecting himself

jokes.text: or as you call it, Pictionary.
jokes.setup: Mentions Charades as entertainment
jokes.punchline: Another unnecessary 'correction' to a completely different game
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation - now he's 'correcting' to wrong information entirely

jokes.text: Homer, for the last time, do not drink the Yahtzee dice.
jokes.setup: Homer is apparently consuming game pieces
jokes.punchline: The specific weirdness of drinking dice, plus 'for the last time' implying repetition
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist Homer moment - perfectly in character with great specificity and implied history

jokes.text: Moving church to Monday afternoon.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions church conflict with game night
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual rescheduling of sacred institution for social event
jokes.explanation: Great Homer logic - treating church like a personal appointment he can reschedule

jokes.text: You'll only taste the spoon.
jokes.setup: Offering 'No Alarm Chili'
jokes.punchline: Chili so mild the spoon has more flavor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders - even his attempt at bold cooking is aggressively bland

jokes.text: couples friends are a myth, started by restaurants with tables for four.
jokes.setup: Marge wants couples friends
jokes.punchline: Homer's cynical conspiracy theory about restaurant seating driving social norms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational comedy - takes common social pressure and creates plausible conspiracy theory

jokes.text: They're always bragging about their trip to Rome. It was 12 years ago, and it was a layover.
jokes.setup: Complaining about the Van Houtens as potential friends
jokes.punchline: The pathetic reality behind their impressive-sounding travel story
jokes.explanation: Great escalating revelation - transforms impressive travel story into pathetic reality

jokes.text: Right now, I'm thinking long novella.
jokes.setup: Lisa claims isolation will fuel her art
jokes.punchline: The contradiction in 'long novella' - novellas are by definition short
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa moment - her precocious vocabulary betrays her actual knowledge gaps

jokes.text: The saddest thing would be if you were driving and texting Lisa and you hit her. And the last thing she texted before she died was, 'I got your message.'
jokes.setup: Marge says Lisa's friendlessness is the saddest thing imaginable
jokes.punchline: Bart's horrifically specific and morbid alternative scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark Bart comedy - escalates darkness with modern tech anxiety and perfect ironic final text

jokes.text: This Taj Mahal should be floated down the Ganges.
jokes.setup: Topiary competition show criticism
jokes.punchline: Treating plant sculpture like a funeral pyre with cultural reference
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist TV criticism - applies funeral rites to topiary competition

jokes.text: Why was that on the Military History Channel?
jokes.setup: Watching topiary competition show
jokes.punchline: Questioning the bizarre programming decisions of specialty cable channels
jokes.explanation: Decent meta observation about cable TV's content drift, relatable confusion

jokes.text: When you're too quiet, you get that psycho look.
jokes.setup: Marge coaching Homer for dinner party behavior
jokes.punchline: Specific observation about Homer's resting face when he's trying to behave
jokes.explanation: Great character detail - Homer's attempt at good behavior makes him look threatening

jokes.text: Walking distance, Marge!
jokes.setup: Marge limits him to one drink
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate appeal based on proximity logistics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - uses safety as excuse to drink more, delivered with desperation

jokes.text: You were charming enough to win me, and that day you didn't have a drink in you.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to drink for confidence
jokes.punchline: Marge's pointed reminder that alcohol wasn't necessary for his greatest achievement
jokes.explanation: Sweet relationship moment with implied criticism - gentle but pointed

jokes.text: You know, I have more cheeks.
jokes.setup: European-style air kissing greeting
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation and offer of additional face real estate
jokes.explanation: Good Homer cultural confusion - treats formal greeting like service opportunity

jokes.text: I believe the mare has a slight limp.
jokes.setup: Elaborately planned murder mystery party with hired sound effects
jokes.punchline: Foley artist adding unnecessary detail to imaginary horse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of party pretentiousness - professional takes job too seriously

jokes.text: Uh, it says here the murderer is Admiral Wainsworth. Who's that?
jokes.setup: Elaborate murder mystery game setup
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately ruins game by reading the solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer move - destroys elaborate planning through innocent incompetence

jokes.text: Birth, school, work, death. Worth, drool, shirk, breath. Mirth, cruel, quirk, meth.
jokes.setup: Woman withdrawing into melancholia after game is ruined
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dark and drug-referencing rhyming variations on life's stages
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating depression spiral - starts philosophical, ends with 'meth'

jokes.text: Well, as we say in Russia, 'Good-bye in Russian!' Do svidaniya!
jokes.setup: Homer playing Russian count character
jokes.punchline: Meta joke about speaking Russian followed by actual Russian
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor - Homer's clunky way of showing off language knowledge

jokes.text: You want to be more alone? Yes.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to be alone after social disaster
jokes.punchline: Marge's incredulous question and Homer's matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good relationship dynamic - Homer's antisocial tendencies revealed

jokes.text: Okay, I slept on the couch and I flipped the sweaty cushions over. What else can I do?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to make up for social disaster
jokes.punchline: His pathetic attempts at damage control including gross cushion detail
jokes.explanation: Good Homer self-awareness - knows he's in trouble but solutions are inadequate

jokes.text: Don't you dare say 'ostrich eyes'!
jokes.setup: Marge uses word 'ostracized'
jokes.punchline: Preemptively stopping Homer's expected pun
jokes.explanation: Great relationship dynamic - Marge knows Homer's terrible pun habits so well she preempts them

jokes.text: Those are past-due bills. They'll get their blood money.
jokes.setup: Discussing Maggie's daycare 'letters'
jokes.punchline: Homer's sinister characterization of childcare payments
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - treating routine bills like extortion payments

jokes.text: Gus Huebner. I was on Lisa's coed soccer team two years ago.
jokes.setup: Only kid who showed up to Lisa's party
jokes.punchline: Pathetic connection from years-old sports team
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - weak social connection highlights Lisa's isolation

jokes.text: Well, Lisa's gonna be here in five minutes, and the only kid who showed up is Gus freakin' Huebner.
jokes.setup: Failed party planning
jokes.punchline: Homer's frustrated emphasis on the disappointing attendee
jokes.explanation: Decent frustration comedy - Homer's contempt for innocent kid

jokes.text: I was the only chump who showed up at this train wreck.
jokes.setup: Kid realizes party situation
jokes.punchline: Self-aware assessment of his pathetic attendance
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware kid humor - acknowledges his own foolishness

jokes.text: I wish. That kid can play youth soccer.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks if he's really Gus Huebner
jokes.punchline: Homer's sincere respect for the kid's athletic abilities
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - unexpected admiration for kid's sports prowess

jokes.text: Marge, this is when I normally eat lunch. So I'll just tell you Lisa is... unique. And we're done.
jokes.setup: Marge asking teacher about Lisa's social problems
jokes.punchline: Teacher's dismissive non-answer to get back to lunch
jokes.explanation: Good teacher indifference - prioritizes lunch over helping concerned parent

jokes.text: if you don't find a dance partner within ten seconds, there is something very wrong with you!
jokes.setup: Square dancing partner selection
jokes.punchline: Teacher's cruel psychological pressure on children
jokes.explanation: Good dark education satire - teacher's inappropriate psychological manipulation

jokes.text: It ain't gym class if a fat kid's not crying.
jokes.setup: Observing square dancing chaos
jokes.punchline: Teacher's callous philosophy about physical education
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - perfectly captures cruel gym teacher mentality with economy

jokes.text: At the risk of sounding like a broken record, yes, yes, yes!
jokes.setup: Tumi invites her to record store
jokes.punchline: Lisa's vinyl-related pun while showing enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Decent Lisa pun - appropriate to context but somewhat forced

jokes.text: Hmm. Nobody likes jazz that much. Even the guy playing it had to take drugs.
jokes.setup: Observing Tumi's jazz enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Cynical observation about jazz musicians and substance abuse
jokes.explanation: Good dark observation - links excessive enthusiasm to musical history of addiction

jokes.text: Oh, no one likes veggie smoothies that much. Or at all.
jokes.setup: Tumi's enthusiasm for healthy smoothies
jokes.punchline: Homer's universal rejection escalating to complete dismissal
jokes.explanation: Decent escalating dismissal - second thought is more damning than first

jokes.text: Don't open the envelope.
jokes.setup: Offers envelope for $5 plus advice
jokes.punchline: The advice contradicts the sale entirely
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic twist - criminal gives honest advice that undermines his own scam

jokes.text: It's for my library card.
jokes.setup: Caught taking money from Lisa's mom
jokes.punchline: Pathetic lie about innocent use for the money
jokes.explanation: Decent awkward lie - library card excuse is both innocent and implausible

jokes.text: You couldn't wait a damn decade until I got into college?
jokes.setup: Lisa confronts Marge about paying for friendship
jokes.punchline: Specific timeline showing she knows friendship will come eventually
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa moment - mature understanding of her social timeline with justified anger

jokes.text: Let me tell you a little story about a chubby unpopular boy named Homer. Is that the boy you named me after? It is you, you idiot!
jokes.setup: Grandpa starts story about 'a boy named Homer'
jokes.punchline: Bart's confusion and Grandpa's exasperated clarification
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff - Bart's literal interpretation creates perfect misunderstanding

jokes.text: I paid a couple of boys, Lenny and Carl, to make my Homer feel loved. A relationship I continued to this day.
jokes.setup: Revealing Homer's childhood friendship problems
jokes.punchline: The shocking revelation that adult friendships are still paid arrangements
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark revelation - recontextualizes decades of Homer's relationships with devastating efficiency

jokes.text: You pay Lenny and Carl to be my friends?! Yes, but Barney's yours for nothing.
jokes.setup: Homer's shock at paid friendship revelation
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's consolation that one friend is genuine (and it's Barney)
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - the 'free' friend being Barney (alcoholic loser) makes it worse somehow

jokes.text: I wouldn't pay ten cents to a lion to stop him from eating you!
jokes.setup: Homer asks if story is true
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's bizarre hypothetical about not paying for Homer's safety
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist insult - specific scenario makes the cruelty more vivid and funny

jokes.text: I'm gonna tell every psychiatrist I ever go to what you did.
jokes.setup: Lisa's anger at Marge's friendship scheme
jokes.punchline: Specific threat about future therapy sessions
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa threat - perfectly in character with her psychological awareness and future planning

jokes.text: Wow, I made Mom cry. What unimaginable power. I can use this to get anything I want. But right now, all I want is for Mom to stop crying.
jokes.setup: Lisa sees the effect of her harsh words
jokes.punchline: Recognition of power followed immediately by remorse and love
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - intellectual calculation immediately overcome by emotional maturity

jokes.text: But when I grow up, I'll find other weird kids, and we will have the most intense relationships ever.
jokes.setup: Making peace with Marge about friendship issues
jokes.punchline: Lisa's mature understanding of how outsiders find each other
jokes.explanation: Great Lisa wisdom - perfectly captures how intellectual misfits eventually connect

jokes.text: Mother's Day?! Mother's Day?! Crap!
jokes.setup: Lisa wishes Marge happy Mother's Day
jokes.punchline: Homer's panicked realization he forgot the holiday
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer panic moment - predictable but in character

jokes.text: Have you ever tried horse meat? They eat it raw in Japan, and...
jokes.setup: Tumi claims to not be really vegetarian
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to disturbing exotic meat consumption
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Tumi's deception - goes from vegetarian lie to extreme carnivore behavior

tmdb_overview: Marge swears off befriending any more couples when Homer offends their charming new British neighbors, but when Lisa declares that she, too, doesn’t need friends, Marge reconsiders.
